title: Operations Executive
description:
Job Description:

Estate & Facility Operations

  • Oversee the upkeep of school buildings as assigned by the Operations Manager
  • Implement preventive and corrective maintenance programs for all key systems (Electrical, HVAC, Plumbing etc)
  • Conduct regular inspections, initiate timely repairs and recommend improvement plans
  • Ensure school infrastructure complies with local building code, safety regulations and MOE Guidelines
  • Address feedback or facility issues promptly and professionally
  • Ensure school is ready for the start of school day

School Operations

  • Oversee and ensure school opens on time
  • Be on ground to ensure smooth traffic flow during morning peak hour period
  • Cover front-desk duties as and when necessary

Security Management

  • Oversee daily security operations and manage outsourced security personnel
  • Oversee drop-off point during the morning peak hour period
  • Manage requests for viewing of CCTV footage
  • Manage staff card access system and issuance of staff access card
  • Manage METRO season parking system and issuance of staff parking car decal

Project Management

  • Plan, coordinate and oversee projects assigned by the Operations Manager
  • Prepare specification documents, evaluation reports and other documentation for Invitations to Quote (ITQ) and Invitations to Tender (ITT)
  • Supervise contractors and worksites to ensure safety, quality and compliance
  • Ensure Quality assurance processes for project completion verification before payment processing

Event & Logistic Management

  • Plan and support logistics (Venue setup/ moving of tables and chairs/ parking) for school events
  • Support logistics for school day to day operations

Requirements

  • Minimum Diploma in Facilities Management of Equivalent
  • Fresh graduates are welcome to apply
  • Candidates without the minimum qualifications but with at least 5 years of relevant prior experience may apply
  • Minimum 3 years of experience in facilities management (including managing outsourced security guards) preferably in a school environment
  • Experience managing renovation, repair and improvement projects, preferably in a school environment
  • Understanding of building systems, maintenance protocols and safety standards
  • Strong communication skills to liaise with stakeholders and vendors
  • Team player and but able to work and lead projects independently
  • Working hour from 6.30am to 4.00pm, Monday to Friday

postedCompany.description: Envisioned to be a global leader in transforming learning, the School of Science and Technology, Singapore (SST) seeks to nurture passionate innovators who improve society through real-world applications of science and technology. If you value forging excellence, building an empowered community and expanding learning networks, talk to us about building a rewarding career at SST!
postedCompany.name: SCHOOL OF SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY, SINGAPORE
